items.blurb: A trio of vinyl bunnies moonlighting as pastry chefs. Stick them on a laptop or bottle and instantly feel 30 percent softer.
Three vinyl polar bears with a serious sweets habit. Durable enough for bottles, cute enough to make strangers ask where you got them.
A tiny sweets-shop box that opens to 20 flake stickers of a bird living its best dessert life. The packaging alone deserves a shelf spot.
Twenty pup-and-pastry flake stickers tucked in a box styled like candy. Journal fuel, gift-topper, or pure desk treat.
A candy-box of 20 cat flake stickers, each one sweeter than the last. Dangerously easy to use all at once.
Twenty round little seal stickers in a box dressed up as confectionery. Guaranteed to soften any planner spread.
A sweets-style box hiding 20 bunny flake stickers in five designs. The kind of tiny purchase that fixes a whole week.
Twenty bear flake stickers packed in a box that looks straight out of a patisserie. Stationery hoarders, look away.
A risograph bird postcard with those happy little ink quirks that make each print one of one. Frame it or actually mail it, both count.
Riso-printed pup postcard where the slightly offset colors are the whole charm. Too cute to send, honestly.
A cat postcard printed the slow risograph way, so every copy is a tiny original. Perfect pen-pal ammunition.
Risograph bunny postcard with softly misaligned colors that feel handmade. Ideal for walls, desks, and long-distance friends.
Two polar bears mid-hug, riso-printed with charmingly imperfect layers. Summer-soft and frame-worthy.
Sweet-shop birds for your planner margins. Half snack, half pun, entirely charming.
A sheet of dogs tangled up with desserts, made for journal spreads that need a giggle.
Cats plus confectionery on one sticker sheet, which is the correct ratio for any planner page.
A seal bobbing through cream sodas and sweets. Instant fizz for a flat journal week.
Bunnies with a bakery agenda, sized perfectly for planners, letters, and gift tags.
Polar bears getting into the sweets drawer, one sticker sheet at a time. Planner catnip.
Die-cut bird sticky notes that turn to-do lists into tiny dessert menus. Reminders were never this soft.
Pup-shaped sticky notes with a sweets streak. Leave one on a coworker's monitor and watch their day improve.
Cat die-cut sticky notes for notes you actually want to look at. Deadline, but make it adorable.
Bunny sticky notes cut into sweet little shapes. Your grocery list deserves this much charm.
A5 sheet of Necktie's deadpan animals puttering around a kitchen. Big stickers, bigger personality.
Animals on urgent bread-delivery business, rendered in Necktie's dry Hokkaido humor. A full A5 of joy.
Necktie's sleepy critters easing into the morning, one oversized A5 sticker sheet at a time.
Sketchy mountain scenes with Necktie's quietly funny animals wandering through. Journal landscaping, sorted.
A little bakery staffed entirely by Necktie's charming animals. An A5 sheet you'll ration like the last croissant.
Ten meters of Necktie's walnut-loving woodland critters on a roll. Wrap it around anything that needs more whimsy.
Forty flake stickers of soft illustrated boys going about small daily moments. Gentle, story-ish, endlessly journalable.
Round-cornered sticky notes in milky coffee tones. Your reminders, now with latte energy.
Four fluffy pastel shades of sticky note in one top-glued pad, corners rounded for maximum softness. Cotton candy, but productive.
A very proper wolf butler tending to daily duties across one sticker sheet. Formalwear on animals: always correct.
A sheep in a butler suit, politely managing your planner's affairs. Impeccable service, maximum fluff.
A dapper dog butler at your service, sticker by sticker. Tea will be served on every journal page.
A fox butler with immaculate manners and a full schedule of tiny chores. Sly, suited, and stickable.
A bear butler keeping the household in perfect order, one sticker at a time. Distinguished and extremely huggable.
Watercolor birds and blooms dressed up like bead embroidery, finished with holographic foil. A jewelry box in sticker form.
Soft beige florals with embroidered-bead detailing and a wink of holo foil. Understated sparkle for grown-up journals.
Fresh green florals styled like tiny stitched keepsakes, glinting with delicate foil. Planner pages, meet heirloom energy.
Dogs, flowers, and fruit sharing one cheerful sticker sheet. Limited run, unlimited serotonin.
Summer scenes built from layered torn washi tape by collage artist Miki Tamura, flattened into stickers. Textured, luminous, lovely.
Botanical wax-seal style stickers in deep red for envelopes and journal pages. Old-world romance, zero melted wax.
Shinako Moriyama's gentle bunnies welcoming spring among the flowers. Soft, painterly, and quietly limited.
A sticker sheet of Moriyama's signature bunnies being their tender, flower-adjacent selves. Bunny people will understand.
Eight die-cut washi notes that fold shut like miniature storybooks. Write something small; deliver something magical.
A finely detailed cat-courier rubber stamp by Megumi Hori, born from a Tokyo stamp-museum event. Special delivery, every impression.
A tiny pin of a cat presiding over a toaster. Breakfast supervision, pinned to your tote.
A pocket-size cat doctor pin, ready to make house calls on your jacket lapel. Prescribed: daily wear.
A little cat pin waiting patiently by the phone. Extremely relatable, extremely wearable.
A bookish cat pin lost in a good story. The right badge for tote bags and fellow bookworms.
A handmade canvas pouch in smoky blue from Okayama craftsmen, built to soften and improve with every year of pen-wrangling.
Moss-green canvas pouch, hand-stitched in Okayama and destined to age beautifully alongside your stationery habit.
The roomier size of The Superior Labor's sunny yellow canvas pouch. Handmade, hardworking, and quietly cheerful.
A cheerful yellow canvas pouch handmade in Okayama, sized for pens, tools, and small treasures that deserve nice housing.
A chocolate-brown canvas pouch from The Superior Labor, handmade to carry your everyday kit and get better looking with age.
Bluebird stickers styled like delicate embroidery, all soft stitches and no needle required. Planner pages, instantly heirloom.
Yuko's storybook nutcracker illustrations in sticker form, sweet enough for December and stubbornly charming year-round.
A swan-lake reverie by illustrator Yuko, turned into stickers with serious ballet-recital elegance.
Toshinori Mori's traveling cats road-tripping through Golden Week Japan on one painterly postcard. Wanderlust, feline edition.
Traveling cats loitering around a sunny Japanese port town. A postcard that smells faintly of sea breeze.
Mori's traveling cats caught in a soft summer rain. The coziest weather report you'll ever pin up.
A limited-run sticker book from illustrator mizutama: 20 sheets you can snip, layer, and scatter however your journal demands.
Tobimatsu's slightly surreal animals living it up in dessert land. Stickers with a wink and a sugar rush.
A swan and a bear sharing a dreamlike lake, per Tobimatsu's gently surreal imagination. Sticker-sheet storytelling at its best.
A birthday card where Tobimatsu's whimsical animals throw the party for you. The kind of card that gets kept.
Gratitude, delivered by Tobimatsu's softly surreal cafe animals. Says thank you better than you were going to.
A postcard ode to the melon parfait, staffed by Tobimatsu's daydreaming animals. Dessert-counter nostalgia in the mail.
Pudding a la mode gets the full Tobimatsu treatment: playful animals, retro cafe vibes, one very sendable postcard.
A postcard portal to a paradise made entirely of sweets and Tobimatsu's cheerful creatures. Send sugar, no shipping issues.
Tobimatsu's animals running a bustling little bakery on one postcard. Fresh out of the oven, straight to your pen pal.
Foil-kissed tea party stickers that shimmer when the light hits. Your planner formally requests your presence.
A foil-stamped flower garden that glimmers across your journal pages. Green thumb not required.
A tiny foil-accented fruit shop, open for business on your planner page. Peak market-day charm.
Shimmery foil stickers with sweet animal details that catch light like tiny treasures. Glitter, but tasteful.
Puffy summer-sweets stickers with foil shimmer. Shaved ice, sunshine, and squish, all limited edition.
A Shiba's night out at the summer festival, captured in limited-edition planner stickers. Fireworks and festival food included.
A seal paddling through cream sodas on limited-run planner stickers. The fizziest way to log your summer.
Twenty washi flake stickers of a seal and its beloved cream soda, printed on soft Mino paper. Summer in sticker form.
A Shiba and a cream soda, riso-printed with those happy accidental color shifts. A limited summer postcard worth hoarding.
